purpose: Machine-readable record of discovery, review, normalization, risk, catalog, and package value events.

events.summary: Installed board-driven heartbeat protocol so Skills operators prioritize discovery restoration, risk review, package-facing skill quality, and catalog parity over status-only churn.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-restore-discovery-handoff-001
events.summary: Consumed the discovery handoff repair ticket, confirmed canonical latest.json is absent while producer workflow/scripts exist, documented the connector-visible blocker, queued a metadata-only manual discovery fallback, and preserved package/risk gates.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-risk-review-mcp-tool-poisoning-001
events.summary: Removed a package-facing risk blocker by converting the MCP/tool-poisoning and SSRF audit skill into a high-risk, requires_review defensive wrapper. Default executable probing, SSRF target lists, unauthenticated endpoint probe commands, and MCP tool-call snippets were removed while preserving authorized static review, fingerprinting, severity scoring, and remediation workflow. Catalog parity is now the next gate.

Verified catalog/install-manifest parity after the MCP/tool-poisoning and SSRF risk review. dist/catalog.json and dist/catalog.md reflect security-defensive/high/requires_review metadata for the skill, and dist/install-manifest.json remains coherent for installer selection. No generated catalog surface was hand-edited and no npm publication was attempted. Manual discovery fallback is now the next value ticket.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-manual-discovery-fallback-001
events.summary: Consumed the manual discovery fallback ticket after the canonical discovery Action handoff remained absent. Recorded four real public metadata-only candidates: modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench, and openclaw/openclaw. Classified three as review_next and one as watch, opened a source-review queue, and preserved no-clone/no-execute/no-copy/no-normalize/no-package/no-publication boundaries.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-source-review-batch-001
events.summary: Consumed the manual source-review ticket. Verified license/redistribution boundaries for modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, and openclaw/openclaw; kept QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench on watch because LICENSE returned 404; created three source profiles, one license-blocked watch note, one high-risk OpenClaw source-runtime review queue, and one bounded Vercel AI SDK normalization queue. No clone, execution, implementation copy, normalization, catalog, package, npm, or publication action occurred.

Consumed the OpenClaw source-runtime risk-review ticket. Classified messaging/account/channel, gateway, browser/canvas/nodes/cron, host-tool, pairing/allowlist/sandbox, daemon, and onboarding surfaces as high-risk; allowed only generic original runtime-surface safety patterns; blocked command snippets, account setup, browser automation, cron execution, operational channel mutation, gateway setup, daemon/onboarding detail, implementation copy, normalization, catalog, package, npm, and publication. Vercel AI SDK normalization is now the next eligible ticket.

Consumed the Vercel AI SDK normalization ticket. Created an original medium-risk, requires_review package-facing skill for provider abstraction and tool/sandbox safety, with source attribution to vercel/ai and no copied third-party examples, prompts, snippets, or implementation content. Because dist skill content changed, catalog parity after normalization is now the next gate.

Verified catalog/install-manifest parity after the AI SDK Provider Tool Safety normalization. dist/catalog.json and dist/catalog.md reflect skill_count 1183, software-engineering 147, medium 434, and the ai-sdk-provider-tool-safety entry; dist/install-manifest.json remains coherent for installer selection fields. No generated catalog surface was hand-edited and no npm/package/registry publication was attempted. The bounded metadata backlog Critic ticket is now the next eligible board item.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-metadata-backfill-batch-001
events.summary: Consumed the bounded metadata-backfill Critic ticket. Classified Autonomy Loop and Autoresearch Loop as agent-operations, medium risk, requires_review skills with source status and review notes. Stopped at Browser Trace after direct review found browser/CDP/session/API-key/DOM/screenshot trace surfaces, opened a high-priority Browser Trace risk-review ticket, and created a blocked catalog parity ticket for the metadata changes. No catalog surface was hand-edited and no publication was attempted.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-risk-review-browser-trace-001
events.summary: Consumed the Browser Trace risk-review ticket. Converted the package-facing browser/CDP trace skill into a high-risk, requires_review, authorized-read-only defensive debugging wrapper; removed local debugger launch, trace capture/query, Browserbase/API-key, and browser-session operational snippets; preserved authorization, minimization, redaction, retention, and safe report guidance. Catalog parity after metadata-backfill and Browser Trace changes is now the next gate. No browser session was opened, no trace tooling was executed, and no publication action occurred.

Verified catalog/install-manifest parity after metadata backfill and Browser Trace risk review. dist/catalog.json and dist/catalog.md reflect the updated counts: skill_count 1183, agent-operations 108, software-engineering 148, high 16, medium 436, uncategorized 560, and unspecified 722. dist/install-manifest.json remains coherent for installer selection fields. No generated catalog surface was hand-edited and no package, npm, registry, or publication action occurred. The next bounded metadata-backfill batch is now the next ready ticket.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-metadata-backfill-batch-002
events.summary: Consumed bounded metadata-backfill batch 002. Classified Code Complexity Scanner as software-engineering low/no-review, Code Review and Code Review Excellence as software-engineering medium/requires_review authorized repository-review skills, then stopped at Diagnosing Bugs after direct review found browser/headless automation and captured trace replay surfaces. Opened a high-priority Diagnosing Bugs risk-review gate and blocked catalog parity behind it. No online discovery, clone, execution, third-party content copy, package, npm, registry, or publication action occurred.

events.source: Skills Value Operator board ticket skills-risk-review-diagnosing-bugs-001
events.summary: Consumed the Diagnosing Bugs risk-review ticket. Converted the package-facing debugging playbook into a medium-risk, requires_review, repository-owner-authorized diagnostic wrapper; classified browser/headless automation, DOM/console/network capture, captured trace replay, real payload fixtures, fuzz, bisection, and HITL surfaces; removed package-facing operational browser/session/trace-capture detail; preserved safe feedback-loop, cause-isolation, fix/verify, and regression-test guidance. Catalog parity after metadata-backfill batch 002 is now the next gate. No online discovery, clone, browser session, trace tooling, third-party content copy, package, npm, registry, or publication action occurred.

Verified catalog/install-manifest parity after metadata-backfill batch 002 and the Diagnosing Bugs risk review. dist/catalog.json and dist/catalog.md show skill_count 1183, software-engineering 152, uncategorized 556, high 16, low 10, medium 439, and unspecified 718. dist/install-manifest.json remains coherent for package-facing selection fields. No generated catalog surface was hand-edited and no package, npm, registry, or publication action occurred. The next bounded metadata-backfill batch is now ready, while publication remains blocked.

events.source: Skills Value Operator Reporter-only missing-ledger repair
events.summary: Initialized the missing 2026-07-09 daily status, queues, and report ledgers from the prior verified state. Per the missing-ledger rule, no board ticket was consumed and no discovery, risk, metadata, catalog, package, npm, registry, or publication work occurred. The next eligible board ticket remains skills-metadata-backfill-batch-003.
